input: I want to compute the mean of a 2D list [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]], then perform modular exponentiation with the mean as the base, 2 as the exponent, and 3 as the modulus.

tools.name: parse_json_log_line
tools.description:
Parses a log line in JSON format and returns a Python object.
If the log line is not valid JSON, returns None.

tools.name: read_file_version
tools.description: Reads the version number from a file.

output_0.description: A tuple containing the major, minor, and patch version numbers.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: flat_list
tools.description: Flattens a nested list of integers or strings into a 1-dimensional list.

lst.description: The nested list to be flattened.

tools.name: process_api_responses
tools.description: Processes a list of API responses and returns a list of key-value pairs, where the keys are the specific API responses and the values are the number of occurrences in the original list.

output_0.description: A list of key-value pairs, where the keys are the API responses and the values are the number of occurrences in the original list.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: create_temporary_json_file
tools.description: Creates a temporary json file and writes a dictionary to it.

dictionary.description: The dictionary to write to the temporary file.

output_0.description: The temporary file object.

tools.name: find_negative_index
tools.description: Returns the negative index of a given number from the end of a sequence.

tools.name: replace_code
tools.description: Replaces all occurrences of the `{code}` variable in a given XML text with the provided code and returns the modified XML text.

xml_text.description: The XML text to search in.

tools.name: coin_toss
tools.description: Simulates a coin toss and returns the outcome.

p.description: The probability of getting a head.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: compute_mean
tools.description: Computes the mean of a two-dimensional list of integers or floats.

two_d_list.description: A two-dimensional list of integers or floats.

output_0.description: The mean of the elements in the two-dimensional list.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: get_concatenated_text
tools.description: Retrieves the text from a JSON file, removes all line breaks, and returns a concatenated string.

json_file_path.description: The path to the JSON file.

output_0.description: A string containing the concatenated text with all line breaks removed.

tools.name: get_ports
tools.description: Returns a list of `n` available ports using lambda expressions and context managers.

n.description: The number of available ports to return.

tools.name: transpose_2D_array
tools.description:
Transposes a 2D NumPy array by switching the row and column indices.

The transposed array has the same data as the original, but its rows and columns
are swapped. The function handles any-sized 2D arrays, and returns `None` if a
non-2D array is passed to it. If the input array is empty, the function returns
an empty array.

output_0.description: The transposed 2D NumPy array, or `None` if the input array is not 2D.

tools.name: modular_exponentiation
tools.description: Calculates the remainder of a number `a` raised to a power `b` modulo `c`.

Calculates the sample skewness of a collection of numbers.

The sample skewness is defined as the third standardized moment, where the second
moment (variance) is used to standardize the data.

output_0.description: The sample skewness as a float.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

output_0.description: True if there is a pair of numbers in the list that sums to the target number, and False otherwise.

tools.name: eucledian_distance

tools.name: skip_first

tools.name: find_intervals

tools.name: ratio_saliency

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

tools.name: format_camel_case
tools.description:
Formats the given input string as a "camelCase" string.

The first letter of each word is capitalized and spaces are removed.
Built-in functions like `str.capitalize`, `str.join`, or `str.title` are not used.

string.description: The input string to be formatted.

tools.name: generate_random_unique_string
tools.description:
Generates a random 32-bit string from the alphabetic characters 'a-z' and 'A-Z' and digits '0-9'.
Calculates the MD5 hash of the string and returns it if it is unique (i.e., it does not match any
previously generated MD5 hashes).

output_0.description: The MD5 hash of the random string.

tools.name: find_first_match_index
tools.description: Returns the index of the first element that matches in both arrays, or -1 if there is no match.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: get_float_elements
tools.description:
Returns a list of float elements from the input list.
If the input list contains non-float elements, they are converted to float.
If the input list contains no float elements, an empty list is returned.

targetList.description: A list of any types of elements.

tools.name: compute_similarity
tools.description: Computes the similarity between two strings, where the similarity is defined as the percentage of matching characters between the two strings.

output_0.description: The similarity as a float between 0 and 1.

tools.name: remove_smallest_largest
tools.description: Removes the smallest and largest numbers from a list of numbers.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: hash_fn
tools.description: A mock hash function that returns a random integer between 1 and 2^32 (inclusive).

tools.name: sum_by_index
tools.description: Calculates the sum of the values of a given index in a collection of dictionaries.

output_0.description:
A list of integers where each integer is the sum of the corresponding index values in each dictionary.

tools.name: apply_hash
tools.description:
Applies the hash function to each object in the list and stores the object in a dictionary if the hash value is less than `k`.
If an object is already present in the dictionary, a `ValueError` exception is raised.

k.description: The threshold value for the hash function.

objects.description: The list of objects that will be processed.

output_0.description: A dictionary that stores the objects for which the hash value is less than `k`.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: add_1
tools.description: Adds 1 to each number in the list.

output_0.description: A list of numbers with 1 added to each number.

tools.name: four_axis_mean
tools.description: Calculates the mean of a four-dimensional input array along the first two axes.

input_array.description: The input array to calculate the mean of.

tools.name: sanitize
tools.description:
Sanitizes a string by replacing non-alphanumeric characters with a space,
removing duplicate spaces, and checking if the string is empty after sanitization.

string.description: The input string to be sanitized.

output_0.description: The sanitized string if it is not empty, otherwise an empty string.

tools.name: normalize_data_list
tools.description: Normalizes a list of data points to a new range.

data_list.description: The list of data points to normalize.

new_max.description: The maximum value of the new range.

new_min.description: The minimum value of the new range.

output_0.description: The normalized list of data points.

tools.name: increment_array_in_place
tools.description: Increments each element in the given array by 1 in-place.

output_0.description: The modified array.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: sum_or_list
tools.description:
Calculates the sum of a list of integers and returns it as a string.
If the sum is greater than 2**31 - 1, it converts the sum to a list of integers and returns the string representation of this list.

output_0.description: A string representation of the sum or the list of integers.

tools.name: generate_subsets
tools.description:
Generates all possible combinations of the given `nums` as a list of lists.
The returned subsets are sorted by the sum of the numbers in the subset
and do not contain duplicate numbers.

tools.name: get_name_from_email
tools.description: Extracts the name from an email address string.

email.description: A string representing an email address.

output_0.description: A string with the name and domain parts separated by a single space.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: extract_last_match
tools.description:
Extracts the last match of a given pattern in a string.

If the delimiter is not specified, the last match of the pattern should be returned.
If the delimiter is specified, the entire string, including the last match, should be returned.

delimiter.description: The delimiter to separate the end of the string from the match.

input: I have a grid cell with centre coordinates (10, 20) and a cell size of 5. I want to generate an HTML anchor tag with the text "Grid Cell" and the URL as the lower-left and upper-right corners of the grid cell.

tools.name: temp_converter
tools.description: Converts a temperature value from celsius to fahrenheit and vice versa.

unit.description: The unit of the temperature value. It can be either 'C' or 'F'.

value.description: The temperature value to be converted.

output_0.description: The converted temperature value.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: generate_triplets
tools.description: Generates a list of unique integer triplets from a given string `s` that satisfies the rule: "i < j < k" and "s[i] < s[j] < s[k]". If no such triplets are found, the function returns an empty list.

output_0.description: A list of unique integer triplets.

tools.name: dict_from_keys_recursive
tools.description: Creates a dictionary from the given list of keys, where the keys are also used as the values.

index.description: The index of the current key in the list.

keys.description: A list of keys to be used as both keys and values in the dictionary.

tools.name: convert_to_v2_0_0_url
tools.description: Converts a URL from version 1.0.0 to version 2.0.0.

output_0.description:
The equivalent version 2.0.0 URL if the input URL matches the pattern,
    otherwise None.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: read_employee_records
tools.description:
Reads information from a JSON file and returns a list of dictionaries.
Each dictionary contains the `id` and `department` fields of an employee record.
The function only includes records where the `department` field's value is not `None`.

path.description: The path to the JSON file containing employee records.

output_0.description: A list of dictionaries containing the `id` and `department` fields of employee records.

tools.name: compute_csi
tools.description: Calculates the critical success index (CSI) given the truth and forecast arrays.

forecast.description: A 2D numpy array containing binary values (0 or 1) indicating the presence or absence of a weather event.

truth.description: A 2D numpy array containing binary values (0 or 1) indicating the presence or absence of a weather event.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: generate_html_anchor
tools.description: Generates an HTML anchor tag with the given URL and text.

text.description: The text to be displayed in the anchor tag.

url.description: The URL to be used in the anchor tag.

output_0.description: A string containing the HTML code for the anchor tag.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: format_diff_minutes
tools.description: Formats a number of minutes as a string with a 1-unit suffix.

diff_minutes.description: A positive integer or float representing the number of minutes.

output_0.description: A string representing the formatted time difference.

tools.name: extract_urls_from_input
tools.description: Extracts a list of URLs from a JSON-like structure.

input_structure.description: A JSON-like structure containing a list of dictionaries with `id` and `url` keys.

output_0.description:
A list of URLs that satisfy the following conditions:
    - The `id` value must be greater than 100
    - The `url` value must contain the substring `https://`

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: get_arg
tools.description: Retrieves the value of an element from an argument dictionary based on its key.

key.description: The key of the element to retrieve.

output_0.description: The value of the element if it is a non-empty string, otherwise None.

tools.name: get_sha256
tools.description: Calculates the SHA-256 hash of a file and outputs it as a string of hexadecimal digits.

tools.name: get_param_value
tools.description:
Retrieves the value of a command-line parameter.

boolean_flag.description: A boolean flag that, when set to `True`, will return the `default_value` if it is not `None`.

default_value.description: The default value for the parameter. If `None`, no default value is available.

param_name.description: The name of the command-line parameter.

tools.name: get_hash_with_largest_id
tools.description: Returns the hash that contains the key "id" with the largest value.

output_0.description: The hash with the largest "id" value, or None if the input list is empty.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: calculate_sum_and_percentage
tools.description: Calculates the sum of a and b divided by c rounded to the nearest integer, and the percentage of d out of c rounded to the nearest integer.

d.description: The number to calculate the percentage of.

output_0.description: A tuple of two integers.

tools.name: ode_model
tools.description: Calculates the dependent variable C at different times using Euler's method.

C0.description: The initial value of the dependent variable C.

r.description: The parameter r in the ODE model.

output_0.description: A list of the dependent variable C at different times.

tools.name: format_as_html
tools.description: Formats a list of tuples as HTML table rows.

data.description: A list of tuples, where each tuple contains a key and a value.

output_0.description: A string formatted as HTML table rows, where for each tuple the key is used as the table header and the value as table data.

tools.name: map_objects_by_id
tools.description: Maps each object's `id` to the object itself.

objects.description: A list of objects, each containing an `id` attribute.

output_0.description: A dictionary mapping each object's `id` to the object itself.

tools.name: replace_chars_with_ascii
tools.description: Replaces each character in a given string with its ASCII character code.

output_0.description: A comma-separated list of ASCII character codes.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: extract_program_name

tools.description: Calculates the lightness value of a color in RGB space.

rgb.description: A 3-tuple of integers representing the red, green, and blue channels within the range 0 to 255.

output_0.description: The lightness value as a floating-point number between 0 and 1.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: is_valid_class
tools.description:
Checks if the given object is a valid class.

A class is considered valid if it has a `__name__` attribute and its name matches the name of the class itself.
If the input object is not a class, the function returns `False`.

output_0.description: A boolean value indicating whether the class is valid or not.

tools.name: top_three
tools.description: Returns the top 3 highest values from a list of integers.

output_0.description:
A list containing the top 3 highest values from the input list.
    If the input list is shorter than 3, the entire list is returned.

tools.description: Converts a phrase to Camel Case.

output_0.description: The Camel Case string.

tools.name: concat_list_str
tools.description: Concatenates a list of strings into a single string with a specified delimiter.

lst.description: The list of strings to concatenate.

output_0.description: The concatenated string.

tools.name: reverse_words_with_link
tools.description: Reverses the words in a string and replaces the first character of each word with a link to the word's Wikipedia page.

output_0.description: A string consisting of the same words as the input string but with each word reversed and the first character replaced with a link to the word's Wikipedia page.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: str_concat
tools.description: Concatenates two strings.

tools.description: Generates a CSS color string from a hex color string.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: generate_frequency_table
tools.description: Generates a frequency table from a list of numbers.

output_0.description: A dictionary that maps each unique number to its frequency.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: is_narcissistic
tools.description:
Checks if a positive integer n is a narcissistic number.

A narcissistic number is a number that equals the sum of its own digits in the nth power.
For example, 153 is a narcissistic number since 153 = 1^3 + 5^3 + 3^3.

n.description: The positive integer to check.

output_0.description: True if n is a narcissistic number, and False otherwise.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: get_length
tools.description: Returns the length of a string `s`.

s.description: The string to get the length of.

input: I want to convert the 2D location (5, 5) on a 10 x 10 grid to a 1D index, then normalise the resulting index.

tools.name: grid_location_to_index
tools.description:
Converts a 2D location on a 10 x 10 grid to a 1D index.

The resulting index ranges from 0 to 99. The 1D index represents the x and y
coordinates of the 2D location, where x is the horizontal axis and y is the
vertical axis.

x.description: The x coordinate of the location.

y.description: The y coordinate of the location.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: normalise_coordinates
tools.description: Normalises the x and y coordinates of a 2D point.

output_0.description: The normalised x and y values.

tools.name: request_api_data
tools.description:
Makes an API request to the provided URL and returns the response as a Python
dictionary. If there is any error, an empty dictionary is returned.

url.description: The URL to make the API request to.

tools.name: sample_exponential
tools.description: Simulates an exponential distribution with a given mean.

mean.description: The mean value of the distribution.

n_samples.description: The number of samples to generate.

output_0.description: A list of random numbers that follow an exponential distribution with the given mean.

tools.name: format_array
tools.description:
Concatenates a list of strings with a given separator.
If the list has only one item, returns it directly without any changes.
If the list is empty, returns an empty string.

separator.description: The delimiter to be used between the strings. Defaults to a comma.

strings.description: A list of strings to be concatenated.

tools.name: create_tasks
tools.description:
Creates a list of tasks from a given list of tuples.

Each task is a list of tuples, created by slicing the input list.
The number of tuples in each task is determined by the `num_tasks` parameter.

num_tasks.description: The number of tuples in each task.

output_0.description: A list of tasks.

tools.name: filter_images_by_channel_and_threshold
tools.description: Filters images based on channel and threshold.

image_tensor.description: A 4D tensor with dimensions of (batch_size, height, width, channels).

threshold.description: A 1D tensor of the same size as the channels dimension of the image_tensor.

output_0.description: A 3D tensor of shape (batch_size, height, width) that contains the filtered image.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: find_overlapping_tuples
tools.description: Finds the overlapping tuples in a list of tuples.

tuples.description: A list of tuples, each containing a start and end time.

output_0.description: A list of tuples that overlap.

tools.name: compute_relative_coordinates
tools.description: Computes the relative coordinates of the elements in one list with respect to a fixed reference point.

coordinates.description: A list of lists, each representing a coordinate.

tools.name: append_extension
tools.description: Appends the extension `ext` to each of the file names and returns the new list.

ext.description: The extension to append to each file name.

tools.name: check_point_on_segment
tools.description: Checks if a point is on a line segment.

p1.description: The first endpoint of the line segment.

p2.description: The second endpoint of the line segment.

output_0.description: A boolean indicating whether the point is on the line segment.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: deep_copy_matching_keys
tools.description: Creates a deep copy of a dictionary that only contains the keys that match the given keys.

keys.description: The keys to keep in the new dictionary.

output_0.description: A deep copy of the dictionary that only contains the matching keys.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: transform_dataframe
tools.description:
Processes a Pandas DataFrame in a specific way.

If `column_check` is `None`, the function does not do anything and simply returns the input DataFrame.
If it is a string, then the function checks if the string is present as the name of a column in the DataFrame.
If so, it returns a new DataFrame with a column appended, containing the same data in the specified column,
but with all values multiplied by 2.

column_check.description: The name of the column to check and process. Default is `None`.

tools.name: harmonic_mean
tools.description: Computes the harmonic mean of a list of numbers.

output_0.description:
The harmonic mean of the numbers in the list. If any number in the list is 0,
    the function returns `inf`.

tools.name: convert_strings_to_integers
tools.description:
Converts a list of strings to a list of integers.
If the conversion is not possible, the function returns None.

strings.description: A list of strings to be converted to integers.

output_0.description: A list of integers or None.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: dict_to_csv
tools.description: Converts a dictionary to a CSV-formatted string.

output_0.description: A CSV-formatted string where each line represents a key-value pair in the dictionary.

tools.name: get_object_representation
tools.description: Returns the string representation of the object in the format "Hi, my name is {{name}} and I live in {{city}}."

tools.name: first_k_items
tools.description:
Returns a list containing the first k items of L.

If L does not have enough items, a custom exception NotEnoughItemsException is raised.

k.description: The number of items to return.

tools.name: even_odd_sums
tools.description: Computes the sum of the even numbers and the sum of the odd numbers in a given list.

output_0.description: A tuple containing the sum of even numbers and the sum of odd numbers.

tools.name: is_all_uppercase
tools.description: Checks if a given string is all uppercase.

output_0.description: A boolean value indicating whether the string is all uppercase.

tools.name: find_all_true_indices
tools.description: Finds all indices in the input list where the value is True.

output_0.description: A list of indices where the value is True.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: find_xyz_indices
tools.description: Finds the indices of all occurrences of the pattern 'xyz' within the string.

output_0.description: A list of indices where the pattern 'xyz' is found.

tools.name: divide_int
tools.description:
Calculates the quotient of two integers, rounded down to the nearest integer.
If the denominator is 0, returns 0.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: generate_signal
tools.description:
Generates a binary signal of given length.
The generated signal consists of randomly generated 1-bit signals,
where each signal has a 10% chance of being a 1 (true) and a 90% chance
of being a 0 (false). The signal is represented as a string of 1s and 0s,
with each character representing a signal.

length.description: The length of the signal to generate.

output_0.description: A string of 1s and 0s representing the generated signal.

tools.name: rearrange_words
tools.description: Rearranges the characters of each word in the input string in such a way that the first character of each word is capitalized, and all other characters are in lowercase.

output_0.description: The rearranged string.

tools.name: str_to_binary
tools.description: Converts a string to its binary representation, where each character is represented by the ASCII value in 8-bit binary form.

string.description: The input string to be converted to binary.

output_0.description: A list of strings representing the binary representation of each character in the input string.

tools.name: pad_buffer
tools.description: Pads a list of integers with zeros until its length is a multiple of a given integer.

lst.description: The list of integers to pad.

n.description: The integer to divide the length of the list by.

tools.name: track_update_durations
tools.description: Tracks the update durations of multiple batches over time.

time_interval.description: The time interval for calculating the average update duration.

output_0.description: A dictionary containing the batch sizes as keys and their corresponding update durations as values.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: count_letters_and_digits
tools.description: Counts the number of letters and digits in a given string.

output_0.description: A tuple containing the number of letters and digits in the string.

tools.name: decimal_integer_to_binary_string
tools.description:
Converts a decimal integer to a binary string using the standard "string of 1's and 0's" format.
Returns an empty string if the input is not a non-negative integer.

n.description: The decimal integer to convert to a binary string.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: csv_format
tools.description:
Converts a list of strings into a string of comma-separated values (CSV) format.
If a string includes a comma, double quotation marks, or a line break, wrap the string in double quotation marks,
with any internal double quotation marks escaped with another double quotation mark.

string_list.description: A list of strings to be converted to CSV format.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: read_serialized_list
tools.description: Reads a binary file containing a list of integers, where each integer is serialized as four bytes.

file_path.description: The path to the binary file.

output_0.description: A list of integers representing the serialized integers from the file.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: scale_vectors
tools.description:
Scales each element in `vector1` with the corresponding element in `vector2`.
If `vector2` is shorter than `vector1`, the function extends `vector2` to the length of `vector1`
by repeating the last element of `vector2`.

output_0.description: A new list with the scaled values of `vector1`.

tools.name: is_valid_coordinates
tools.description: Checks if the values in a 2D list of strings are valid coordinates.

matrix.description: A 2D list of strings representing a matrix.

output_0.description: A boolean value indicating whether the values in the matrix are valid coordinates.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: create_score_dict
tools.description: Creates a dictionary where the keys are taken from the `names` list and the values are taken from the `scores` list.

output_0.description: A dictionary where the keys are names and the values are scores.

tools.name: list_of_strings
tools.description: Returns a list of strings based on the configuration provided.

tools.name: replace_in_quotes
tools.description: Replaces certain text in a string with a given replacement string, but only if the text is surrounded by quotation marks.

replacement_text.description: The replacement text. If not provided, the original text will be returned.

tools.name: transform_dict_list
tools.description: Transforms a list of dictionaries into a list of keys and a list of values.

output_0.description: A list of tuples containing the values corresponding to the keys in the input dictionaries.

tools.name: invert_permutation
tools.description: Inverts a permutation of integers.

permutation.description: A permutation of integers from 0 to the length of the array minus 1.

output_0.description: A new array which inverts the permutation.

tools.name: default_str
tools.description: Returns the input string if it is not empty, otherwise returns "default".

tools.name: replace_seven
tools.description: Replaces all occurrences of 7 with 1 in a given string.

output_0.description: The input string with all occurrences of 7 replaced with 1.

tools.name: macro_sub
tools.description:
Replaces all occurrences of {macro} in the string with the specified macro value.
If a macro name is not found in the list, leave it as {macro} in the result.

macros.description: A dictionary of macro name, value pairs.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: count_bytes
tools.description: Calculates the number of bytes needed to encode a string as ASCII.

output_0.description: The number of bytes needed to encode the string as ASCII.

tools.name: parse_and_assign
tools.description: Parses a list of strings and creates a dictionary where each key is a key name and each value is the corresponding value.

data.description: A list of strings of the form `key1:value1,key2:value2,key3:value3,…`.

output_0.description: A dictionary where each key is a key name and each value is the corresponding value.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: list_all_files
tools.description: Recursively traverses a file system directory and returns a list of all files, including their absolute paths.

directory.description: A string representing the directory path to traverse.

output_0.description: A list of strings representing the absolute paths of all files in the directory and its subdirectories.

tools.name: replace_route_with_path
tools.description: Replaces the substring "route" with "path" in the given error message.

output_0.description: A new error message with "route" replaced with "path".

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: compute_vector_distance
tools.description: Calculates the Euclidean distance between two 1-dimensional vectors.

output_0.description: The Euclidean distance between the two vectors.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: extract_second_number
tools.description:
Extracts the second number from a string that contains two numbers separated by a space.
If the pattern does not match, uses a fallback value of 0.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: random_item
tools.description: Chooses a random item from a list. If the input list is empty, returns None.

tools.name: compute_degree

The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.description:

If the list is empty, returns 0. If the list contains only one element, returns it.

tools.name: get_mean_and_variance
tools.description:
Returns the mean and variance of the list `nums`.
If `nums` is empty, returns `None`.

tools.name: count_unique_substrings
tools.description:

Also handles the case where the input string is `None`, `s` is empty, or `k` is greater than the length of the input string.

k.description: The length of the substrings to be considered.

tools.description: Extracts the size of an image specified in a PNG file's header.

Converts a JSON file to a list of objects with a single key-value pair for each line.
The key name and its value are the same for each object.

json_file_path.description: The path to the JSON file.

output_0.description: A list of objects with a single key-value pair for each line of the input JSON file.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: map_boolean_to_binary_representation
tools.description: Maps a boolean value to a value based on the binary representation of the boolean.

boolean_value.description: The boolean value to be mapped.

output_0.description: The mapped value.

tools.name: delete_empty_keys
tools.description: Deletes the keys with empty values from a dictionary.

output_0.description: A new dictionary with all the keys that have an empty value removed.

tools.name: boolean_to_string
tools.description: Converts a boolean value or NoneType object to its corresponding string representation.

value.description: The boolean value or NoneType object to convert.

output_0.description: The string representation of the input value.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: md5_hash_time
tools.description: Computes and returns an MD5 hash of the current system time in milliseconds, represented as a hexadecimal string.

output_0.description: A hexadecimal string representing the MD5 hash of the current system time in milliseconds.

tools.name: ratio_saliency
tools.description: Calculates the saliency ratio for a given time series.

time_series.description: A list of integers representing the time series.

output_0.description: A list of floats representing the saliency ratios.

tools.name: parse_list_of_numbers
tools.description: Parses a string representing a list of numbers (separated by commas) into a list of floats.

output_0.description: A list of floats.

tools.name: count_words_from_sentences
tools.description: Counts the words in a list of sentences.

output_0.description: A dictionary of words and their corresponding counts.

tools.name: is_ancestor
tools.description: Determines whether a given node is an ancestor of another node in a tree represented as a dictionary.

ancestor.description: The name of the node to check if it is an ancestor.

descendant.description: The name of the node to check if it is a descendant.

output_0.description: True if the first node is an ancestor of the second node, False otherwise.

tools.name: sort_keys_alphabetically
tools.description: Sorts the keys of a dictionary in alphabetical order, where the keys are first filtered by their first letter being a lowercase letter.

output_0.description: A list of the sorted keys.

tools.name: generate_bit_patterns
tools.description: Generates all possible bit patterns of length `n`.

n.description: The length of the bit patterns.

output_0.description: A list of all possible bit patterns, where each pattern is represented as a string consisting of '0' and '1' characters.

tools.name: non_whitespace_substrings
tools.description: Returns a list of non-empty substrings that do not contain any whitespace characters.

input_str.description: A string containing multiple words separated by whitespace characters.

tools.name: traverse_diagonal
tools.description: Traverses a square matrix and returns the diagonal elements, ordered from the top-left to the bottom-right.

matrix.description: A square matrix represented as a list of lists.

output_0.description: A list of the diagonal elements.

tools.name: camel_case
tools.description: Converts a string with underscores into camel case.

output_0.description: The camel case version of the input string.

tools.name: fluctuate_position
tools.description: Generates a random number `r` between `-delta` and `delta`, and then returns `x + r`.

x.description: The horizontal coordinate of a 2D point.

tools.name: concatenate_one_by_one
tools.description: Concatenates a string one character at a time using a for loop.

string.description: The input string to be concatenated.

output_0.description: The concatenated string.

tools.name: compute_degree
tools.description:
Computes the degree of a polynomial expressed as a list of coefficients in decreasing order of powers.
The function handles a leading zero case where the degree of the polynomial is 0 (i.e., the list only contains a single zero).

coefficients.description: The coefficients of the polynomial in decreasing order of powers.

tools.name: image_to_base64
tools.description: Converts an image file to a base64-encoded string.

path_or_bytes.description: The file path or BytesIO object of the image.

output_0.description: The base64-encoded string of the image.
